ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Tom Yum Crispy Fish Noodle Soup; closest parent 'noodle soup food product' (FOODON_00002473) used as the nearest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW (Noodle soup) is the closest defensible parent category; no specific Tom Yum crispy fish noodle soup entry exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for Tom Yum Crispy Fish Noodle Soup; SNOMED CT does not enumerate individual dish-level food items at this specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Tom Yum Crispy Fish Noodle Soup is approximately 450 g, comprising a bowl of spicy-sour Tom Yum broth with noodles and a portion of crispy deep-fried fish.

nutrient_source.title: Tom Yum Noodle Soup (estimated composite) — adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for noodle soup components and FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition data for Thai dishes
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FAO INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Tom Yum Crispy Fish Noodle Soup as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by component analysis: egg noodles (USDA SR Legacy #20109), fish (deep-fried, USDA SR Legacy #15264 adapted for frying oil uptake), Tom Yum broth components (lemongrass, chilli, fish sauce, lime juice) cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA Thai food composition tables. Values represent a plausible per-100 g composite for a restaurant-style bowl (approx. 450 g total: ~120 g noodles, ~80 g crispy fish, ~250 g broth with vegetables). Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of noodle-based soups — analogous estimate based on egg noodles (GI 46–54, mean 50) and rice noodles (GI 53–61) from the University of Sydney GI Database; composite dish GI estimated at 52 accounting for protein and fat co-ingestion attenuating postprandial glucose response.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Tom Yum Crispy Fish Noodle Soup. GI estimated at 52 (low category) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entries for egg noodles (GI ~50, tested) and rice noodles (GI ~53–61). The presence of protein (crispy fish, ~6.2 g/100 g) and fat (~3.8 g/100 g) in the composite dish is expected to attenuate the glycemic response relative to plain noodles. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 49.5) / 100 ≈ 25.7, where carbohydrate per 450 g serving = 11.0 × 4.5 = 49.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce, shrimp paste, and broth
hypertension.guidance: Tom Yum Crispy Fish Noodle Soup contains approximately 480 mg sodium per 100 g (≈2,160 mg per 450 g serving), which represents over 90% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size, request reduced fish sauce, and avoid drinking the broth in full.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from noodle carbohydrates; protein and fat co-ingestion partially attenuates postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated glycemic index of 52 (low) but a glycemic load of approximately 25.7 per standard serving, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size of noodles, consider substituting rice noodles with shirataki or reduced-carbohydrate alternatives, and pair the dish with additional non-starchy vegetables to further blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and deep-frying oil uptake in crispy fish component
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high sodium content (~2,160 mg per serving) and the saturated fat contribution from deep-frying the fish (~4.1 g saturated fat per serving) are relevant cardiovascular risk factors; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption, request steamed or grilled fish as a substitute where available, and avoid consuming the full volume of broth to reduce sodium intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tom Yum Crispy Fish Noodle Soup (Thai: ต้มยำปลากรอบราดบะหมี่; romanized: Tom Yam Pla Krob Rat Ba Mi) is a Thai noodle soup dish combining the bold, aromatic flavours of Tom Yum broth with deep-fried crispy fish served over egg or rice noodles. The broth is prepared by simmering lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, Thai chillies, shallots, and mushrooms in a fish-sauce-seasoned stock, then finishing with lime juice to achieve the characteristic spicy-sour-savoury balance. The crispy fish — typically a whole small fish or fish fillet deep-fried until golden and crunchy — is placed atop or alongside the noodles and broth, providing a textural contrast between the crunchy exterior and the tender, flaky interior. The dish is popular in Thai restaurants across Southeast Asia, including Singapore, where it is served as a complete one-bowl meal. Nutritionally, a standard 450 g restaurant serving provides approximately 441 kcal, 27.9 g protein, 17.1 g fat, and 49.5 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is high (~2,160 mg per serving) due to fish sauce and broth concentration, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low category), though the glycemic load per serving is moderate (~25.7), relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes who should monitor noodle portion size. The dish is dairy-free and generally halal-suitable when prepared without pork-derived ingredients.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Tom Yum Crispy Fish Noodle Soup as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by component analysis using USDA SR Legacy and FAO/INFOODS SEA data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for noodle-based dishes. Medium confidence reflects the estimation approach; values are plausible and internally consistent but not derived from a direct measurement of this specific dish.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated composites; no direct approved-source entry for this specific dish was found. Values should be updated if HPB Singapore Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA publishes a direct entry for Tom Yum noodle soup with crispy fish. 2. The following micronutrients are set to -1 (sentinel value: not reported in source) because they are not reliably reported in the component-level sources used for this estimation and cannot be defensibly assigned without a direct composite analysis: vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used; vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used; vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used; vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used; vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used; vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used; folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used; vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used; vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used; phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used; magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used; zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used; selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used. 3. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0; this is a confirmed trace/negligible value consistent with non-hydrogenated frying oils typical in Thai cooking. 4. The food_name has been normalized from the menu-style label 'T9 Noodle Tom Yum Crispy Fish Soup' to the canonical English form 'Tom Yum Crispy Fish Noodle Soup'; the original menu name is preserved in aliases. 5. Halal status is assumed based on typical preparation without pork; restaurant-specific halal certification should be verified. 6. GI estimated at 52 based on University of Sydney GI Database noodle entries; glycemic load per 450 g serving = (52 × 49.5) / 100 ≈ 25.7.
